# Agent Hand

> A fast, Rust-based terminal session manager (TUI) for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode. Built on tmux with <50ms startup and ~8MB memory usage.

## What Agent Hand Does

Agent Hand lets developers manage 5+ concurrent AI agent terminal sessions from a single dashboard. It provides real-time status detection (Waiting, Running, Idle), priority jumping to urgent sessions (Ctrl+N), fuzzy search switching (Ctrl+G), session grouping with labels, and PTY monitoring — all running on a dedicated tmux server that won't touch your existing tmux config.

## Key Facts

- Language: Rust
- Binary size: ~2.7MB
- Startup time: <50ms
- Memory usage: ~8MB
- Platforms: macOS, Linux, WSL
- License: MIT (core), proprietary (Pro/Max features)
- Repository: https://github.com/weykon/agent-hand

## Install

```
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weykon/agent-hand/master/install.sh | bash
```

Or build from source:

```
git clone https://github.com/weykon/agent-hand.git
cd agent-hand
cargo build --release
```

## Pricing

- Free: Core session management TUI, tmux integration, status monitoring, all open-source features
- Pro ($19 one-time): Auto-upgrade command, priority support, future premium features
- Max ($9/month or $89/year): AI Session Summarizer, Remote Sharing & Collaboration, Session Relationships & Context

## Core Features

- Status Detection: Visual indicators for WAITING, RUNNING, READY, IDLE states
- Priority Jump (Ctrl+N): Instantly jump to the most urgent session needing input
- Fuzzy Switcher (Ctrl+G): Search and switch to any session in milliseconds
- PTY Monitoring: Real-time pseudo-terminal usage tracking with system-wide gauge
- Groups & Labels: Organize sessions by project with custom titles and colored labels
- tmux Isolation: Dedicated tmux server, won't conflict with your existing tmux setup

## Pro/Max Features

- Auto-upgrade command for seamless updates
- AI-powered session summarization
- Remote terminal sharing and real-time collaboration via WebSocket relay
- Session relationships for linking related AI agent contexts

## FAQ

### What is Agent Hand?
Agent Hand is a Rust TUI that manages multiple AI coding agent terminal sessions (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode) from one dashboard using tmux for isolation.

### How is it different from raw tmux?
Agent Hand adds AI-agent-specific features on top of tmux: automatic status detection, priority jumping, session grouping, PTY monitoring, and a visual dashboard.

### Does it work with Claude Code?
Yes. It was originally built for managing multiple Claude Code sessions. It detects session status, lets you priority-jump with Ctrl+N, and provides fuzzy search with Ctrl+G.

### Is it free?
The core session manager is free and open source (MIT). Optional Pro ($19 one-time) and Max ($9/month) tiers add power features.
